purpose: Code/config/platform changes that affect nx.dev GA measurement or traffic, for correlating against the daily/weekly/monthly series. Dates are when the change took EFFECT in production where known (astro-docs from the Linear update + GA ramp); otherwise the master-merge date, noted as such (nx.dev deploys from a website-NN branch, so prod can lag master).

events.label: GTM added
events.expected_impact: GTM container added alongside direct GA. No visible effect on Views (baseline disproves doubling).

events.label: astro-docs /docs live
events.expected_impact: CONFIRMED prod date (Linear 'live on nx.dev/docs' + GA /docs ramp Sep 29-30). Docs content moves from root paths to /docs; before this, docs sat in Others. astro is a different framework, so docs never had the next.js double-count.

events.label: server_page_view starts
events.expected_impact: First server-side page_view (Measurement Protocol) in GA (58,683 that day). Sent by the Netlify edge function, so its start ~coincides with the Vercel->Netlify cutover. Separate event, NOT in Views.

events.label: Vercel -> Netlify (DNS)
events.expected_impact: CONFIRMED prod date: DNS cutover from Vercel to Netlify. Whole site moves hosting; Netlify edge functions (Framer proxy, server_page_view) go live. First infra change in the decline window - a candidate alongside the GTM-only cutover for the Feb-Apr client-views decline.

events.label: GTM-only cutover
events.expected_impact: SUSPECT for the broad pre-Cookiebot decline. Removed the explicit gtag('config') page_view; page_view now depends on the GTM container config (shared lib used by next.js AND astro-docs). The container's 'send a page_view on load' toggle was found OFF, and Consent Mode defaults can withhold hits - so capture became config-dependent. This is a config effect, NOT ad-blocking: gtm.js and gtag/js are blocked equally (both on googletagmanager.com, both in EasyPrivacy), so the gtag->GTM switch does not change ad-block susceptibility. Merged to master 2026-02-10; prod (website-22) likely late Feb/early Mar; GA decline onset ~mid-March.

events.label: Framer migration
events.expected_impact: Homepage + marketing pages move to Framer (proxied). NOT the decline cause on its own (docs declined too, and docs is not on Framer).

events.label: nx-blog standalone
events.expected_impact: CONFIRMED prod date: BLOG_URL env var set in Netlify prod 2026-04-16 10:08 AM, so /blog + /changelog proxy to the standalone Astro blog (nx-blog). Code (#35043) merged to master Mar 27 but is on NO website-NN branch - the switchover was the env-var flip. Part of the content-migration-off-next.js sequence that degraded client tracking.

events.label: nx-dev pared down
events.expected_impact: next.js nx-dev pared to /ai-chat, /api, /courses only - the rest of the site is now Framer/Astro. Completes the move of content off the next.js app (which carried the GTM page_view tracking).

events.label: bot-probe 404s
events.expected_impact: Bot-probe short-circuit + /pricing routing. Minor on client views.

events.label: Cookiebot banner
events.expected_impact: MAJOR: client-side GA consent-gated -> the big May client-views cliff.
